# The missing UI for Claude Code

Run parallel Claudes safely in containers. Jump between their environments to instantly test changes. Get suggestions that catch critical issues as you go.

Download Sculptor

Email me a download link

* * *

## Preserve craft while coding with AI

Start with your ideas and iteratively refine them, just like traditional software engineering. You're the architect while AI agents handle the implementation details.

[Get the docs](https://github.com/imbue-ai/sculptor)

![Spin up parallel agents](/static/parallel-agents-ac8d6f0192da6b575005401e71bbe66a.png)

### Spin up parallel agents

Each Claude works in its own container. You get safe execution and parallel agents without the hassle of git worktrees.

![Instantly test agent changes](/static/instantly-pair-d0b4bc629d9146f1be7d0930e8200813.png)

### Instantly test agent changes

Run, test, and edit agent changes in your dev environment. Switch between agents with Pairing Mode.

![Merge without conflicts](/static/merge-f3c7f16c8450713cf46eed9700091655.png)

### Merge without conflicts

Merge the changes you like and throw out the ones you don't. Sculptor helps you resolve merge conflicts.

Download Sculptor

Email me a download link

I've been moving more and more of my coding off of Cursor and on to Sculptor btw.

The vibes are good, and the experience has been pretty nice.

* * *

Sculptor lets me maintain this level of craftiness to software development without losing the edge you get from AI tools.

Jed McCaleb

CTO, Stellar

* * *

Wow, this is slick!!

Dale Hamilton

Staff Engineer, Universe

* * *

At first I thought, 'why do I need this container?'. But when I realized Sculptor was actually solving the pain of concurrent agents on different branches, it made total sense.

It's like—oh wow I don't have to manage that mess anymore.

Patrick Sulin

Technical Lead, InterSystems

* * *

The killer feature for me is parallelization. I can kick off multiple tasks at once without spinning up a whole new environment every time. It feels like the tooling is finally here to support the kind of workflows I've always wanted.

Robert Starmer

CEO, Kumulus Technologies

* * *

I compared Claude Code running in Max Mode with Sculptor, and Sculptor's results and overall intelligence were better. I've already merged around 5K lines—it's a great product! Kudos to you guys.

Andrew Gabriel

Founder, Monostate.ai

* * *

## Come sculpt with us!

Hop into our Discord to share feedback, report bugs, and chat directly with the Imbue team. You'll find a community of developers exploring workflows, sharing ideas, and uncovering what coding agents can really do.

[Join our Discord](https://discord.gg/GvK8MsCVgk)

## Built with Sculptor: Community highlights

* * *

![Dale used Sculptor to build [a foreign-language journaling app](https://lingolog.app). While Sculptor handled the refactors, fixed build issues, and churned through background tasks, he spent his time painting the landing page. In his words:
“In a world where generative art is booming, I used Sculptor to write code so that I could go make art.”  ](/static/lingolog-c8a56c56ae708979a057a7f4e9ffc919.png)

Dale used Sculptor to build [a foreign-language journaling app](https://lingolog.app). While Sculptor handled the refactors, fixed build issues, and churned through background tasks, he spent his time painting the landing page. In his words:

“In a world where generative art is booming, I used Sculptor to write code so that I could go make art.”

Dale Hamilton

Engineer

* * *

Jitendra created a tool that analyzes Spotify profiles and generates personalized playlists using the Eleven Labs music API. What began as a fuzzy idea quickly became a working prototype—Sculptor's parallel agents made it easy to explore and bring the project to life.

[@Dunskt](https://x.com/Dusknt)

Forward Deployed Engineer, Eleven Labs

## [FAQ](#faq)

### Do you support other coding agents aside from Claude Code?

Not yet—support for GPT-5 is on our roadmap. Ping us on [Twitter](https://x.com/imbue_ai) or join our [Discord](https://discord.gg/GvK8MsCVgk) to request others!

### Why containers over git worktrees?

Containers let each agent install packages and run code without risking your machine. They also unlock Pairing Mode, so you can hop instantly between agents in your local dev environment to test changes ([see demo](https://www.loom.com/share/1b02a925be42431da1721597687f7065?sid=009bbd6c-fc59-4943-b783-3803c224b75f))—unlike git worktrees, which require environment setup every time.

### How much does it cost to use Sculptor?

Sculptor is free while we’re in beta. You’ll just need Anthropic access—we support API keys or your Claude Pro/Max plan.

### What data does Sculptor collect?

Sculptor runs fully locally, and you control what data it sends back to Imbue.

*   Essential only → shares just error logs and basic usage (safe choice for work code).
*   Standard → adds chat messages (but not code or file contents).
*   Full contribution → adds logs with code and authorizes training, which helps us improve Sculptor's ability to verify agent work.

Sculptor never trains on or views your code unless you're at Full contribution. You can switch levels anytime in settings.

### What platforms does Sculptor support?

You can use Sculptor on macOS and Linux today, and on Windows through WSL (Windows Subsystem for Linux).

### Can I contribute to Sculptor or extend it?

Right now, Sculptor is still in closed development. But our goal is to build an open, community-driven ecosystem, and extensibility is on the roadmap. If you'd like to shape what that looks like, ping us on [Twitter](https://x.com/imbue_ai) or join our [Discord](https://discord.gg/GvK8MsCVgk)!
